metadata.document: 2026 AHA/ASA Guideline for Early Management of Patients With Acute Ischemic Stroke
metadata.purpose: Maps clinical topics to guideline sections. The LLM classifies the user's question into a topic from this list. Python looks up the section. The topic IS the routing — no keyword matching.

usage.llm: Reads the topic list + 'addresses' descriptions to classify the question. Picks one topic. Optionally picks a subtopic qualifier.
usage.python: Looks up topic → section. If qualifier provided, narrows to subtopic section. Direct lookup, no scoring.

topics.addresses: Public education for stroke symptom recognition (FAST, BE-FAST), community awareness campaigns, reducing time to 911 activation
Emergency medical services in stroke care — dispatcher recognition, prehospital notification, transport protocols

Prehospital stroke scales (LAMS, RACE, CPSS, FAST-ED, VAN), field triage for large vessel occlusion, prehospital blood pressure and glucose, IV access, EMS dispatch tools, advance hospital notification
Which hospital to transport to — bypass decisions, drip-and-ship vs mothership, primary vs comprehensive stroke center, interhospital transfer
Mobile stroke unit (MSU) vehicles with onboard CT — deployment, impact on treatment times, outcomes of MSU-based care
Stroke center certification levels — primary stroke center (PSC), comprehensive stroke center (CSC), thrombectomy-capable, acute stroke ready
ED protocols for rapid stroke evaluation — door-to-imaging times, stroke team activation, laboratory workup, code stroke
Telestroke for remote neurological assessment, thrombolysis decision-making, hub-and-spoke models
Regionalization of stroke care, interfacility transfer protocols, system-wide coordination
Stroke registries (GWTG-Stroke, Paul Coverdell), quality metrics, performance measures, benchmarking
Clinical stroke severity tools — NIHSS, modified Rankin Scale, use in treatment decisions and prognostication
Brain and vascular imaging for acute stroke — CT, CTA, CT perfusion, MRI/MRA, ASPECTS, penumbra/core mismatch, LVO detection
Laboratory workup — blood glucose, troponin, ECG, coagulation studies, platelet count, echocardiography, cardiac monitoring
Airway management, supplemental oxygen, intubation indications in acute stroke
Head-of-bed elevation vs flat positioning in acute stroke
BP targets before and after thrombolysis/thrombectomy, antihypertensive agents, permissive hypertension, hypotension avoidance
Fever treatment, therapeutic hypothermia, antipyretics in acute stroke
Hyperglycemia and hypoglycemia management, glucose targets, insulin protocols in acute stroke
Intravenous thrombolysis — eligibility, agent selection, time windows, contraindications, special populations
Endovascular thrombectomy — patient selection, timing, techniques, special populations

subtopics.qualifier: adult patients (time windows, ASPECTS, vessels, large core)

topics.addresses: Early antiplatelet after AIS — aspirin, DAPT (aspirin + clopidogrel), ticagrelor, timing after IVT, glycoprotein IIb/IIIa inhibitors
Acute anticoagulation in AIS — heparin, LMWH, argatroban, early vs delayed anticoagulation for AF, dissection, DVT
Volume expansion, hemodilution, vasodilators, induced hypertension in acute stroke
Neuroprotective agents (including prehospital neuroprotection) — past failed trials and current investigational agents (nerinetide, uric acid, magnesium)
Emergency carotid endarterectomy (CEA), carotid stenting (CAS) in acute stroke or crescendo TIA
Organized stroke unit care — staffing, monitoring, outcomes vs general wards
Swallowing screening and management after stroke — aspiration prevention, instrumental evaluation
Nutritional assessment and enteral feeding for stroke patients
Venous thromboembolism prevention — pharmacological (LMWH, heparin) and mechanical (compression, IPC)
Screening and treatment of depression after stroke
Falls prevention, urinary care, skin care, infection prevention, palliative care, general medical management
Early rehabilitation — timing, intensity, PT/OT/speech therapy, discharge planning
General approach to cerebral edema after large infarction — monitoring, goals-of-care, transfer decisions
Post-stroke seizure management — acute treatment, antiseizure medication, prophylaxis evidence

Post-IVT and post-EVT monitoring and management — Table 7 monitoring protocol, BP targets after thrombolysis/thrombectomy, antiplatelet timing after IVT, stroke unit admission, neurological monitoring, complication surveillance. NOTE: There is no single guideline section for post-treatment management; this content is assembled from multiple sections.
Table 8 — absolute contraindications, relative contraindications, and 'benefit may exceed risk' situations for IV thrombolysis
